title: Robin Reads the Path to Pluton
description: On pages 7–11, this part focuses on robin reads the path to pluton, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Robin Reads the Path to Pluton. Crocodile drags the conflict toward the royal tomb and the truth of Pluton while Robin watches the plan unfold. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: to the top of the top

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Is this how the Royalist Scums Fight!?

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: How low are you willing to sink to!?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Everyone, please! You have to stop fighting!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: The first time I got the time to

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: But your voice won't reach them now...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: So this is as far as we can get...!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The dust clouds... They're your doing.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But as long as I stop that explo- sim 15 minutes from now,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: "I can stop the rebellion if I do this..."

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: "I can stop it if I do that..."

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Your Unbear- Able Idealism is sick- ending!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ideals can only be brought to reality by people with ability...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: What do I care if you find it unbearable?

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: I won't abandon my ideals no matter what!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Someone like you could never understand!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: I'm the princess of this country! I'll never give up to the likes of you!

summary: This part develops robin reads the path to pluton and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
